column2: IEEE stands for {{c1::Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers}}.
column4:
Why

The acronym matters because Objective 2.2 discusses IEEE wireless standards, especially 802.11.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column9: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Cloze IEEE Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column3: Wi-Fi generation names are alternate names for specific 802.11 standards.
column4:
Memory Trick

The ac standard maps to the earlier Wi-Fi 5 generation name.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column9: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Cloze HighYield WiFi Standards Source::Messer-v170

column2: 802.11ax is also known as Wi-Fi {{c1::6 and 6E}}.
column3: The approved source associates 802.11ax with Wi-Fi 6 and Wi-Fi 6E.
column4:
Exam Trap

Do not treat Wi-Fi 6E as a completely different 802.11 base standard from 802.11ax in this objective.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column9: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Cloze HighYield WiFi Standards Source::Messer-v170

column4:
Source Note

This card tests the standards/generation mapping only, not product availability or vendor support.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column9: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Cloze WiFi Standards Source::Messer-v170

column2: 802.11 Wi-Fi commonly uses {{c1::2.4 GHz}}, {{c2::5 GHz}}, and {{c3::6 GHz}} frequency ranges.
column3: Some environments and technologies may use more than one frequency range.
column4:
Why

The band names are stable, high-value wireless recall. Exact regional spectrum endpoints are intentionally not carded here.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column9: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Cloze HighYield WiFi Frequencies Source::Messer-v170

column3: Bluetooth is commonly used for nearby peripherals such as headsets, speakers, keyboards, and mice.
column4:
Exam Trap

Bluetooth and Wi-Fi can both be wireless, but Bluetooth is typically a short-range peripheral technology.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column9: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Cloze Bluetooth Frequencies Source::Messer-v170

column3: RFID is commonly used for access badges, inventory tracking, and other identification workflows.
column4:
Memory Trick

The key word is “identification”: RFID tags help identify or track things.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.8; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.2
column9: A+::220-1201::2.2 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::WirelessNetworkTechnologies Cloze RFID Acronym Source::Messer-v170
